articles.title: CLICKOPS
articles.summary: npx workos@latest launches an AI agent, powered by Claude [5], that reads your project, detects your framework, and writes a complete auth integration into your codebase. No signup required. It creates an environment, populates your keys, and you claim your account later when you're ready.

articles.title: S3 FILES AND THE CHANGING FACE OF S3
articles.summary: AWS S3 Files is a new feature that integrates Amazon EFS with S3 to resolve "data friction" when moving large datasets. It enables S3 buckets to be mounted as network-attached file systems on EC2, containers, or Lambda. Instead of merging file and object semantics, the design uses an explicit "stage and commit" boundary to optimize for distinct access patterns, broadening S3's capabilities beyond simple object storage.

articles.title: PARSE, DON'T VALIDATE — IN A LANGUAGE THAT DOESN'T WANT YOU TO
articles.summary: The "Parse, don't validate" principle makes sure of type safety by transforming raw input into precisely typed forms that encode validity directly into the system. This approach establishes a clear boundary between untrusted data and trusted domain types, reducing errors and the need for defensive programming.

articles.title: RUNNING OUT OF DISK SPACE IN PRODUCTION
articles.summary: A production server quickly ran out of disk space due to Nginx buffering large files, causing significant system errors and user complaints. The issue was resolved by disabling proxy buffering and setting the maximum temporary file size to zero, which successfully restored stable disk usage.

articles.title: CLAUDE IS NOT YOUR ARCHITECT. STOP LETTING IT PRETEND
articles.summary: Don't let AI agents make architectural decisions. They're agreeable pattern-matchers that produce generic designs without understanding your team's real constraints, and nobody owns the result when things break. The fix is to have humans design and AI implement.

articles.title: HOW I RUN A SOFTWARE ENGINEERING STANDUP
articles.summary: Daily standups are necessary for fully-remote software engineering teams and should be held daily and early with mandatory attendance, starting with a brief ice-breaker. The meeting should follow a time-boxed, random-order format of "Yesterday, Today, Blockers" to prioritize the identification of obstacles.

articles.title: CLERK BILLING NOW SUPPORTS SEAT-LIMITED PLANS
articles.summary: Building B2B SaaS? Clerk Billing lets you define org membership limits per plan — Free up to 10 seats, Pro up to 50, unlimited on Enterprise. Clerk enforces the limit automatically and prompts members to upgrade when they hit it. No custom logic required.

articles.title: CLICKY
articles.summary: Clicky is an open-source AI teacher for macOS that functions as a cursor-side "buddy," capable of seeing the screen, talking, and pointing at elements for real-time interaction. The system streams audio and screenshots to AI (Claude) via a Cloudflare Worker.

articles.title: XILEM
articles.summary: Xilem is an experimental, high-level reactive UI framework for Rust that allows developers to create lightweight view trees for both web and native backends. Inspired by React and SwiftUI, it uses the Masonry toolkit to provide a performant and scalable foundation for building modern graphical user interfaces.

articles.title: ASSESSING CLAUDE MYTHOS PREVIEW'S CYBERSECURITY CAPABILITIES
articles.summary: Claude Mythos Preview is a new language model with unprecedented capabilities for autonomously discovering and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities across critical software, even creating complex exploits that previously took human experts weeks to develop. Anthropic urged defenders to immediately use current AI models, shorten patch cycles, and fundamentally re-evaluate security strategies to cope with the accelerating pace of vulnerability discovery and exploitation.

articles.title: PROJECT GLASSWING: SECURING CRITICAL SOFTWARE FOR THE AI ERA
articles.summary: Project Glasswing, an initiative led by Anthropic and major tech companies like AWS, Google, and Microsoft, aims to secure critical global software. The project uses Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview, an AI model capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities better than most skilled humans, to proactively find and patch thousands of high-severity flaws in essential systems.

articles.title: CLOUDFLARE TARGETS 2029 FOR FULL POST-QUANTUM SECURITY
articles.summary: Recent quantum computing breakthroughs have accelerated the timeline for "Q-Day," when existing encryption methods will become vulnerable. Cloudflare is responding by expediting its post-quantum roadmap to achieve full security, including crucial authentication systems, across its entire product suite by 2029.

articles.title: BUILD ON BLUEHOST VPS FOR PREDICTABLE, FIXED-COST INFRASTRUCTURE POWERING MILLIONS OF WEBSITES AND AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Predictable, dedicated infrastructure for AI agents, apps, and

articles.title: workloads that need to scale. See which plan works for you.
BINARY OBFUSCATION THAT DOESN'T KILL LTO: A RECAP OF MY THOTCON TALK
articles.summary: This technical blog goes over advanced software engineering solutions for binary security, performance optimization, dynamic analysis, graphics API standardization, and cross-platform compilation.

articles.title: WHAT IS THE AI AGENT LOOP? THE CORE ARCHITECTURE BEHIND AUTONOMOUS AI SYSTEMS
articles.summary: An AI agent boils down to a simple while loop: the model reasons, calls a tool, checks the result, and repeats until the job's done.

articles.title: GITHUB COPILOT CLI COMBINES MODEL FAMILIES FOR A SECOND OPINION
articles.summary: Rubber Duck is an experimental feature in GitHub Copilot CLI that uses a second AI model from a different family to independently review coding agent plans and implementations.

articles.title: WHAT TO KNOW IN JAVASCRIPT (2026 EDITION)
articles.summary: This 2026 JavaScript overview shows new language features from ECMAScript 2025/2026 and developments across its broader ecosystem.
